feed.title: SAP Community - PLM EAM/Plant Maintenance
feed.subtitle: PLM EAM/Plant Maintenance Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: How to Track Approval Details of Maintenance Requests
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

Based on business roles & business catalog, we can determine which users have the authority to approve or reject maintenance requests.

However, when using the "Screen Maintenance Requests" app, it seems that we can only approve or reject requests, but there is no visible approval history or details (e.g., which users have approved the requests).

Is there any way to check this information? Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Single Cycle Plan
entry.content:
1. When creating a single cycle plan is there an option to pick a strategy?

2. Is difference between a single Cycle plan and a Quality plan? 

3. What is single cycle plan mostly used for?

entry.title: Update the commitment item for multiple PM orders in bulk
entry.content:
I want to update the commitment item for multiple PM orders in bulk. Normally, I can open each order one by one in the IW32 screen and update it via Go To → Account Assignments → Funds Management, but I want to develop a program for bulk updates. However, the BAPI_ALM_ORDER_MAINTAIN BAPI does not have a field for this update.

How can I perform the bulk update?

entry.title: Modification of accounting accounts for their allocation from the maintenance order
entry.content:
Hello Experts, I have a question regarding the implementation of the PM module or has the following case arisen in any company or do you think it is possible?

The scenario is about being able to modify the accounting account of the material from the maintenance order,

That is, if a material example: X2569 - points to the accounting account that has the name "Masonry" and is used in maintenance order #1 of a "civil engineering" service, it is correct. 
But if in order #2 the same material X2569 is required but this service is for "Plumbing" that in the maintenance order it can be modified and the corresponding accounting account can be placed which will affect in this case the accounting account called "plumbing".

Is this possible if I have a wide catalog of accounts depending on the maintenance specialty?

Thank you very much in advance for instructing me.
Best regards
Ana

entry.title: Restriction in PO Change after released
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

After release, the user should not be able to change the PO header text for a Purchase Order (PO). If any PO text is changed, the PO should automatically be unreleased at all levels.

How can we implement this restriction in SAP?

PLM Enterprise Asset Management (EAM)/Plant Maintenance (PM) SAP Plant Connectivity MM (Materials Management)

entry.title: How to Configure "standard text key" for task list & Operation
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

How can i configure "standard text key" for equipment task list IA01 & Maintenance Order IW31.

entry.title: Change properties of Attachment Component on Create Maintenance Request [F1511A] via Adaptation
entry.content:
Hi experts, 

I am trying to update the properties of the Attachment Reuse Component that is included on the standard Create Maintenance Request application. I want to update the flavor to "withCheckin" and remove the "Add a Link" action. I have created an adaptation project in BAS and I have tried the following: 

  • Creating an extension controller, attaching onto an event, getting the component, and attempting to change the properties. 
  • Adding a change to the manifest app variant.

Unfortunately, I have had no luck. I can technically update the properties of the component via the extension controller, but my changes are overridden once the component entirely loads in. If I could find a way to set the properties after the component is entirely loaded in then that would work. 

Another option is adding a change to the manifest app variant. I was able to successfully do this on "My Maintenance Requests" but that's much more straight forward since its a true Fiori elements application. 

Example of what I am trying to accomplish:

entry.title: MEASUREMENT POINT MAINTENANCE PLAN DUE BASED ON LAST COMPLETION
entry.content:
We have maintenance plans set up to generate a PM based on a counter reading. 

MP1 - Weekly hour meter reading for counter 1 is entered. 

MP2 - PM generated for equipment every 800 hours based on counter 1. 

At 800 hours MP2 generates a PM for the technician to service the equipment. However, based on equipment availability and scheduling the equipment has 850 hours when the service is completed. 

Is it possible to add a measurement reading to MP2 for hour meter reading once service is completed so that MP2 next service would be at 1650 hours instead of 1600?

entry.title: how to create time-based Condition Monitoring Plan
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

How to create time-based Condition Monitoring Plan or can strategy be used in Measuring point to create Strategy based plan.

entry.title: Automatically creating a suboperation or another operation when an operation is added in SAP PM
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I’m working with SAP PM Work Orders and I need to configure a scenario where adding a specific operation in a work order (IW31/IW32) will automatically create a suboperation or another operation.

My question is:

• Is there a standard SAP way to achieve this without ABAP development?

I appreciate any insights or guidance from those who have dealt with similar requirements.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Change Documents in IW32
entry.content:
Hello Guys, Good Morning!

When i'm creating a External Operation in IW32, all the steps are saved in the Change Documents, but, in some steps, the TCODE column stays empty, when should show the Tcode. 

In IW32 (Extras -> Documents for Order -> CHANGES)

Any clue why this could happen ?

Thanks!!

entry.title: Maintenace plan Automatic Skip FM
entry.content:
Hi Community,

My customer was using standard task list due to this some of maintenance plans calls have no operation so due to this issue when we run RISTRA20 program plan was not scheduled due to No operation error, to avoid this issue we have developed Automatic skip program by using This FM MPLAN_SKIP_CALL. How this program will work is will Schedule Two step job first we will run RISTRA20 2nd step is Automatic Skip program will run once we got error in Ristra 20 step that particular call this automatic program will skip it was skipping successfully,

But the problem when we run this Automatic skip program, Next planned counter reading is not taken as counter confirmation reading, even though we have activated check box for completion requirement. But it is working fine while doing Manul skip in IP10. But when we use This automatic program skipping counter calculation is wrong.

Can you please suggest if you ever encounter this issue and how did you resolve 

Please find below snapshot for better understanding

Best Regards 

Subba Rayudu

entry.title: Standard System T-Codes run slowly
entry.content: Hi all, The customer has SAP 2023. After 2023 upgrade, when add equipments standard tcodes (IW31, IW32,IW41,IW42IW38 etc.), system run too slow. When I checked ST12, the problem due to database. I shared systems result. Also I compare two tables. IFLOT and IFLOT_HIER. IF I add a FL IFLOT_HIER, system run normal. How can update this table? This method solved the problem, but I am not sure this is exact solution. Also I checked with Z structure, and Z tables, There is no problem in the system in these structures.

entry.title: fill Component list from maintenance plans work orders
entry.content:
Hello all,

I Need some help Please?

I am looking for a way to be able to have my components tab already filled out with parts required to completed the task for all of my maintenance work orders created by a certain maintenance plan.

Many Thanks!

entry.title: How to get all system status assigned to any application like equipment, Functional Location etc.
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

How can we get all system status assigned to any Application like Equipment, Functional Location, Notification, maintenance order etc.

entry.title: Availability check on MO with open OBD
entry.content:
Available qty: 2 EA

MO requirement qty: 2 EA

When the MO is created, the ATP commits the MO line with the available qty.

Once Outbound delivery is created for the MO, the material is allocated to OBD. ATP availablity check decommits the MO line.

entry.title: Need Assistance – Auto Unrelease PO on Any Change
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

My requirement is that if any changes are made to a released Purchase Order (PO), it should be automatically unreleased. This should apply to all types of changes, including modifications to the header text and line-item text.

I have identified the following USER EXIT for this requirement. Could you please confirm whether it will work or suggest an alternative if needed?

 Use User Exit (EXIT_SAPMM06E_013)

  1. Inside this enhancement, use the exit: EXIT_SAPMM06E_013
    • If the PO is released by looking at im_ekko-frgke. If the status equals 'G', it indicates the PO is released
    • If header text is modified (EKKO- TDLINE).
    • If change is detected (i.e. the texts differ), reset the release flag by setting EKKO-FRGKE to 'R' (or any value that represents an unreleased state).
    • Change should automatically “unrelease” the PO at all levels
  2. How to find the TDLINE main point. So that I below mentioned the explain & use the function module for read the text.
    • ID=F01
    • LANGUAGE=EN
    • NAME= 410000102400010  (PO NUMBER & ADD 00010)
    • OBJECT NAME=EKPO

 

 

                        

    If TDLINE can be changed the po automatically unrelease all levels.

The new header text (new_txt, assumed to be provided in im_ekko-tdline) is compared with the fetched text (old_txt).

MM (Materials Management) PLM Enterprise Asset Management (EAM)/Plant Maintenance (PM) MAN Material Requirements Planning

entry.title: Restricting Duplicate Notifications for Same Equipment
entry.content:
If a notification is created, first check whether a previous notification exists for the same equipment. If an open notification already exists, restrict the creation of a new one notification.

To implement this logic, I have tried using a user exit with the procedure mentioned below. Can you suggest whether this logic will work smoothly?"

User Exit and Logic for Restriction

In the QQMA0002 & QQMA0026   user exit you will use the VIQMEL table to query existing notifications and restrict new ones based on equipment.

User Exit: (QQMA0002 & QQMA0026):

Steps to Implement the Restriction Logic:

  1. Query the VIQMEL table
    • Use VIQMEL-EQUNR (Equipment Number) to find existing notifications.
    • Filter by QMART (Notification Type) is necessary.
    • Retrieve OBJNR (Object Number) from the result.
  2. Check Status in JEST Table
    • Use OBJNR to fetch the status from JEST table.
    • Filter with INACT field:
      • If INACT is blank (Active) → Restrict notification creation.
      • If INACT is 'X' (Inactive) → Allow creation.
  3. Prevent Notification Creation
    • If restriction applies, use MESSAGE statement to show an error message.

                                                     ⬇️ 

             Already have created same equipment Notification

 

Logic Description (Step-by-Step):

  1. Find Existing 'M2' Notifications: Retrieve all notifications from the VIQMEL table where EQUNR matches the equipment number and QMART is 'M2'.
  2. Check Status (JEST): For each notification found in step 1, retrieve the corresponding status from the JEST table using the OBJNR.
  3. Determine Activity: For each JEST entry:
    • If INACT is blank (space), the notification is considered active.
    • If INACT is 'X', the notification is considered inactive.
  4. Apply Restriction:
    • If at least one active 'M2' notification is found in step 3, prevent the creation of the new 'M2' notification and display an error message.
    • If all existing 'M2' notifications are inactive (INACT = 'X') or no 'M2' notifications exist (step 1 returned no results), allow the creation of the new 'M2' notification.

PLM Enterprise Asset Management (EAM)/Plant Maintenance (PM) SAP Asset Performance Management MM (Materials Management)

entry.title: Error "Serial number already exists for material" when creating new equipment
entry.content:
Hello, 

We are working on a scenario where fresh material is received with an external serial number successfully in stock. 

Then I'm trying to create a new equipment and assign the received material with the external number to it, but it returns an error - "Serial number already exists for material" 

Is there a way to assign already received material with serial number to new equipment?

entry.title: Installation of equipment to functional location via maintenance order possibility
entry.content:
Hello, 

I have a request if there is a possibility to perform an installation of equipment to functional location via corrective maintenance order? 

There is an equipment created with assigned serialized material and successful installation can be performed with IE4N or the respective Fiori app, however i`m trying to perform it via corrective maintenance order and looking for relevant information for it. 

 

Thank you
